summary: ORDI is the first BRC-20 token, deployed on the Bitcoin blockchain via the Ordinals protocol in March 2023 as a fully fair-launch experiment with no pre-mine, no investor allocation, no team reserve, and no foundation treasury. All 21,000,000 tokens were made available for open minting by anyone on a first-come, first-served basis (1,000 ORDI per mint, capped at 21,000 mints total) until the supply was exhausted within days of deployment. Because there were no vesting schedules, lockups, or time-gated allocations of any kind, there are zero structured unlock events to report.
caveats: TokenUnlocks.app does not list ORDI because it has no vesting schedule to track (confirmed by absence of a project page as of mid-2025). Messari free profile (https://messari.io/asset/ordi) and CoinGecko (https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/ordi) both confirm the fair-launch, no-pre-mine structure. The original BRC-20 deploy inscription can be verified on-chain via the Ordinals explorer at https://ordinals.com and the deploy transaction details at https://ordiscan.com/brc20/ORDI. No private sale, VC round, or team allocation documentation exists because none occurred. The only caveat is that large individual wallets that accumulated ORDI during the free-mint window could exert informal sell pressure, but this is holder concentration risk, not a scheduled unlock event, and individual wallet data is NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER without a paid on-chain analytics subscription.
